title: System and method for customizable link between two entities
abstract: A system and method improved communication system and method to permanently create and maintain private connections between entities such as two people, businesses, and/or other types of organizations and entities whereby to create an account with the example communication system, a user may complete an online personal contact application, such that once the information is verified, the user may be assigned a Unique Identification.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A communication system, comprising: one or more databases connected via a network; one or more processors connected to the one or more databases; and at least one computing device connected to the one or more processors and the one or more databases via the network, wherein the one or more processors are configured to: associate a first user with a first unique identifier, wherein the first unique identifier comprises a sequence of alphanumeric characters unique to the first user; associate a second user with a second unique identifier, wherein the second unique identifier comprises a respective sequence of alphanumeric characters unique to the second user; and generate a communication link, wherein the communication link is a stable link and stored in the one or more databases, wherein the contact information of the first user and the second user are stored on the one or more databases wherein when the contact information is updated, responsive to the communication link, the contact information converted into a compatible format for multiple computing devices of the first user and the second user and transmitted to the multiple computing devices of the first user and the second user. 2. The communication system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are configured to transmit filtered content of the contact information to the second user wherein 

selection criteria for the presentation to the second user is elected by the first user. 3. The communication system of claim 2, the communication link comprising the first unique identifier and the second unique identifier and one or more operative tags. 4. The communication system of claim 3, the operative tags including one of a geographic stamp, Internet Service Provider, or time stamp. 5. The communication system of claim 2, wherein the one or more processors are configured to transmit the first unique identifier to one or more second computing devices registered with the first user. 6. The communication system of claim 5, wherein the one or more processors are configured to download the filtered content of the contact information of the second user to the one or more second computing devices registered with the first user. 7. The communication system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are configured to share one or more data files associated with the first user or the second user between the first user and the second user only once the communication link has been generated. 8. The communication system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are configured to: associate an end-user account with an account number received; wherein the first unique identifier is configured to act as an authorization code for authenticating an identity by an institution, wherein the first unique identifier is configured to operate as private key used for a created association with an identity authentication for the associated account number of the first user for the institution. 9. The communication system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are configured to: calculate account activity of the second user, the account activity determined by a time stamp of a last update or confirmation of contact information data by the second user. 10. The communication system of claim 9, wherein the one or more processors are configured to calculate if account activity of the second user has dropped below a predetermined threshold determined from the time stamp, wherein if the account activity has dropped below a predetermined threshold, the one or more processors are configured to update a status of an account associated with the second unique identifier. 11. The communication system of claim 10, wherein the one or more processors are configured to generate a status indicator for the second user, the status indicator displayed to the first user, the status indicator modified in response to the account activity of the second user, wherein if the account activity of the second user has dropped below a predetermined threshold, the one or more processors are configured to transmit a request to the first user to request the second user to update the contact information of the second user. 12. The communication system of claim 11, wherein the status indicator is categorized by color, the color selected is in response to the account activity of the second user, the contact information transmitted to the one or more computing devices updated with the color. 13. The communication system of claim 1, wherein the one or more processors are configured to: associate the first user with a business identifier linked to a related business entity of the first user, wherein the business identifier comprises a sequence of alphanumeric 

characters unique to the business, and wherein the business identifier is permanently linked with the business entity and removably linked with the first user. 14. A communication system, comprising: one or more databases connected via a network; one or more processors connected to the one or more databases; and at least one computing device connected to the one or more processors and the one or more databases via the network, wherein the one or more processors are configured to: associate a first user with a first unique identifier, wherein the first unique identifier comprises a sequence of alphanumeric characters unique to the first user; and associate the first user with a business identifier linked to a related business entity of the first user, wherein the business identifier comprises a sequence of alphanumeric characters unique to the business, wherein the business identifier is permanently linked with the business entity and removably linked with the first user wherein when removed a status indicator is transmitted on all computing devices of one or more other users. 15. The communication system of claim 14, wherein in response to the status indicator, the contact information of the first user on the computing devices altered. 16. The communication system of claim 14, wherein the one or more processors are configured to request approval from the business entity for the business identifier to be 

associated to the first user, wherein in response to the approval of the business identifier, another unique identifier is linked to the first user. 17. The communication system of claim 14, wherein the one or more processors are configured to associate a second user with a second unique identifier, wherein the second unique identifier comprises a respective sequence of alphanumeric characters unique to the second user, and wherein the one or more processors are configured to generate a communication link, wherein the communication link is a stable link and stored in the one or more databases, wherein the contact information of the first user and the second user are stored on the one or more databases wherein when the contact information is updated, responsive to the communication link, the contact information converted into a compatible format for multiple computing devices of the first user and the second user and transmitted to the multiple computing devices of the first user and the second user. 18. The communication system of claim 14, wherein the one or more processors are configured to: generate a packet configured for transmission to at least one institution, the packet comprising the first unique identifier; and associate an end-user account with an account number received, wherein the first unique identifier is configured to act as an authorization code for authenticating an identity by the institution, wherein the first unique identifier is configured to operate as private key used for a created association with an identity authentication for the associated account number of the first user for the institution; 

 authorize the first unique identifier for identification purposes, wherein when an account of user has been verified a page is presented confirming identification; and present instructions to an authorization computing device. 19. The communication system of claim 18, wherein the one or more processors are configured to transmit advertisements from a vender to the first user in response to the first unique identifier associated with a service unique identifier, the service unique identifier corresponding to a service related to the vendor. 20. The communication system of claim 18, wherein the one or more processors are configured to receive the first unique identifier as authentication for step verification.
